questions.q: How many Vedas are traditionally counted in Hindu scripture?
questions.explanation: The four Vedas are the Rigveda, Yajurveda, Samaveda, and Atharvaveda.

questions.q: Which of the four Vedas is regarded as the oldest and is composed chiefly of hymns of praise?
questions.explanation: The Rigveda is the oldest Veda, consisting mainly of suktas (hymns) addressed to the deities.

questions.q: The Vedas belong to which category of Hindu scripture, traditionally regarded as 'that which is heard'?
questions.explanation: The Vedas are classified as shruti, meaning 'that which is heard' by the rishis.

questions.q: Which Veda is especially associated with melody and the chanting of hymns set to music?
questions.explanation: The Samaveda is the Veda of melodies (saman), arranging Rigvedic verses for musical chanting.

questions.q: Which Veda primarily contains the prose formulas and procedures used by priests in performing the sacrifice?
questions.explanation: The Yajurveda supplies the yajus, the ritual formulas and instructions for conducting the yajna.

questions.q: In the Vedic worldview, what is the central ritual around which much of the Vedas is organized?
questions.explanation: The yajna, the fire sacrifice, is the central ritual that much of the Vedic literature serves.

questions.q: Which fire-god is invoked in the very first hymn of the Rigveda as the divine priest of the sacrifice?
questions.explanation: The Rigveda opens with a hymn to Agni, the god of fire who carries offerings to the gods.

questions.q: The Vedas are traditionally described as apaurusheya, which means they are believed to be what?
options: Authorless and not of human composition
questions.explanation: Apaurusheya means 'not of human origin'; the Vedas are traditionally held to be authorless and eternal.

questions.q: By what method were the Vedas chiefly preserved and transmitted across many generations?
questions.explanation: The Vedas were preserved through precise oral recitation passed from teacher to student over millennia.

questions.q: Which warrior-king and slayer of Vritra is the deity most frequently addressed in the hymns of the Rigveda?
questions.explanation: Indra, the storm-king who slays the serpent Vritra, receives the greatest number of Rigvedic hymns.

questions.q: The Vedas are divided into four textual layers. Which of these is one of those four divisions?
questions.explanation: Each Veda is divided into Samhitas, Brahmanas, Aranyakas, and Upanishads.

questions.q: Which layer of Vedic literature forms the philosophical conclusion of the Vedas and is also called Vedanta?
questions.explanation: The Upanishads, the final layer, are called Vedanta, meaning 'the end of the Veda'.

questions.q: The sacred ritual drink frequently praised in the Rigveda, and also personified as a deity, is known by what name?
questions.explanation: Soma is both the ritual drink central to Vedic sacrifice and a deity to whom an entire mandala is devoted.

questions.q: Which Veda is distinct from the others in containing hymns, spells, and charms for healing and everyday life?
questions.explanation: The Atharvaveda includes hymns and incantations for healing, protection, and household concerns.

questions.q: The revered Gayatri Mantra, addressed to the solar deity Savitr, is drawn from which Veda?
questions.explanation: The Gayatri Mantra is a verse from the Rigveda (Mandala 3) addressed to Savitr, the sun.

questions.q: According to tradition, which sage is credited with compiling and dividing the Vedas into four collections?
questions.explanation: The sage Veda Vyasa is traditionally credited with arranging the eternal Vedas into the four known collections.

questions.q: In the Rigveda, which deity is the guardian of cosmic and moral order (rita) and the lord of the waters?
questions.explanation: Varuna is the Vedic god who upholds rita, the cosmic order, and is associated with the waters and the heavens.

questions.q: The famous Purusha Sukta, describing the cosmic being from whose sacrifice the world is formed, appears in which Veda?
questions.explanation: The Purusha Sukta is found in Mandala 10 of the Rigveda, describing creation from the cosmic Purusha.

questions.q: Which portion of Vedic literature mainly explains the rituals and gives prose commentary on the proper conduct of sacrifices?
questions.explanation: The Brahmanas are prose texts that explain and interpret the rituals and sacrifices of the Samhitas.

questions.q: The 'forest treatises' of the Vedas, meant for hermits and forest-dwellers, are known by which name?
questions.explanation: The Aranyakas, or 'forest texts', form the layer between the ritual Brahmanas and the philosophical Upanishads.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, the princes of Ayodhya including Rama are described as being instructed in which body of knowledge?
options: The Vedas and the use of arms
questions.explanation: In the Ramayana, Rama and his brothers are educated in the Vedas alongside the martial arts.

questions.q: The core hymn collections of each Veda, the foundational verses themselves, are called by which term?
questions.explanation: The Samhita is the collection of mantras and hymns that forms the core of each Veda.

questions.q: The Rigveda Samhita is traditionally organized into how many books, or mandalas?
questions.explanation: The Rigveda Samhita is arranged into ten mandalas (books) containing over a thousand hymns.

questions.q: The 'family books', mandalas attributed to specific rishi lineages, form the oldest core of which Veda?
questions.explanation: Mandalas 2 through 7 of the Rigveda are the 'family books', its oldest layer composed by specific rishi families.

questions.q: The Yajurveda is traditionally preserved in two main recensions known by which pair of names?
questions.explanation: The Yajurveda survives in the Shukla (White) and Krishna (Black) recensions.

questions.q: The Nasadiya Sukta, the celebrated 'Hymn of Creation' pondering the origin of existence, is found in which Veda?
questions.explanation: The Nasadiya Sukta, contemplating creation before being and non-being, appears in Mandala 10 of the Rigveda.

questions.q: The six auxiliary disciplines that support the proper study and recitation of the Vedas are collectively called what?
questions.explanation: The six Vedangas (such as phonetics, grammar, and metre) are the limbs of the Veda aiding its correct study.

questions.q: Which Vedanga is the discipline of phonetics and pronunciation, essential for reciting the Vedas accurately?
questions.explanation: Shiksha is the Vedanga of phonetics and pronunciation, ensuring the sacred sounds are uttered correctly.

questions.q: The Samaveda is largely drawn from the verses of which other Veda, set to melody?
questions.explanation: Most verses of the Samaveda are taken from the Rigveda and arranged for melodic chanting.

questions.q: In the Puranas, the Vedas are sometimes said to have been rescued by Vishnu in which of his avatar forms?
questions.explanation: In the Puranas, Vishnu as Matsya, the fish, recovers the Vedas stolen by a demon and restores them.
